§6Carbon Dioxide§f

§aA polluting waste gas with a crucial role — cooling your ZIRNOX reactor!§f

§bCarbon Dioxide§f (CO2) is a polluting gas that mainly occurs as a waste product in petrochemical and material refinement processes. Its primary use is as a coolant in the §bZIRNOX§f reactor, which can consume up to §e16,000 mB§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f — 1,000 mB §bNatural Gas§f → 1,000 mB §bCO2§f (3s, 100 HE/t)
§b• Electrolysis§f — §bAlumina§f (200 mB) → 100 mB CO2 + Aluminium Powder + Fluorite
§b• Fractioning Tower§f — §bCoker Gas§f (100 mB) → 75 mB CO2 + 25 mB Aromatics
§b• Combination Oven§f — §bLimestone Powder§f → CO2 + Calcium Powder
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f — §bCoal Tar§f → 1,000 mB CO2 + Fine Soot

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• §eZIRNOX Coolant§f — the only use; trial and error needed to find the ideal amount for your fuel load

§eTip§f: More CO2 is not necessarily better for the ZIRNOX. Experiment with different amounts to find the sweet spot for your specific fuel configuration. The Chemical Plant route (Natural Gas) is the simplest for bulk production!
